capability: structural-reveal discrimination — isolate a line whose bond joins two previously-separate regions of the entity graph
claim: A bond between two STANDING entities sitting in separate graph components is a structural reveal; the interior connectivity channel (bridgeSurprise) fires maximally on it and ~0 on surface-matched controls (adjacent re-bond, fresh entity), while the mass-based one-surprise (bayesBits) does NOT isolate it (ranks fresh-mass lines above it); the connectivity reading is invariant to a surface emphasis cue.
stimulus_shape: text · discrimination+invariance · L3 · whole-reading · target CON
verdict: CONFIRMED — controls dark (adj=0, fresh=0), bridge unique-argmax on the reveal (1.0), bayes argmax elsewhere (gap real), surface-invariant (unmarked==marked)
mechanism: connectivity collapse over the CON/SIG bond graph: a bond's endpoints in different components -> 1, same-component geodesic delta -> (delta-1)/Dinf, adjacent -> 0; endpoints resolved through the CAUSAL SYN-merge identity quotient (the engine's own union-find, restricted to sentIdx<=cursor) so a reveal that names a standing entity by a new surface form is seen
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new src/core/bridge.js, exported from src/core/index.js beside surpriseAt; wired into src/perceiver/reading.js as an OPT-IN channel readingAt({bridge:true}) so the default path is byte-identical
root_cause_of_gap: the mass one-surprise (surprise.js) has no atom for connectivity — a bond among standing entities is a tiny mass deposit; the structural convergence leaves no trace in the figure field. The existing diagnostic (scripts/reveal-discrimination.mjs bridgeSurprise) located this but lived only in the read-only harness AND keyed on raw surface ids, so it MISSED short-name reveals and faked a 2-hop separation on adjacent controls.
parity: npm test 649->658 (9-test lock added), default readingAt byte-identical (bridge opt-in)
generalization: 194/194 lines of the original reveal-stimulus.json reproduce the prior raw-id bridge column EXACTLY (no regression); coref-resolution is a strict superset — identical where reveals use full names, strictly better on short-name reveals
omnimodal: CONFIRMED on a pure-operator log (no text/parser): cross-component bond -> 1.0, adjacent -> 0, SYN-coref reveal -> 1.0 — the mechanism reads only operators, so any organ emitting CON/SIG/SYN inherits it
scope_holds: any modality whose organ emits bonds (CON/SIG) and identity merges (SYN); structural reveals that join graph regions; reveals naming a standing entity by a new surface form
scope_fails: a reveal carried by NO bond (a pure predication/contradiction leaves connectivity unchanged — the enacted significance loop's province); convergence the weak SVO parser does not draw as a bond or shared object (front-end limit, not the channel's)

capability: the novelty reserve as a SIGNAL-DERIVED amplitude — the reserved mass for an unseen atom tracks the gamma-decayed RATE of recent first-appearances instead of the hand-rolled constant NOVELTY_RESERVE
claim: a rate-derived reserve (nu = Sum_{f<at} gamma^(at-1-f) over first-appearance steps) improves predictive surprise where novelty is positively autocorrelated (bursts) and regresses it where novelty is anti-correlated (maximally-spaced newcomers); the same mechanism cuts both ways because it tracks the RATE of newcomers but is blind to their AUTOCORRELATION
stimulus_shape: text · dissociation · L2 prediction · whole-reading · two novelty regimes (burst/drought vs spaced)
verdict: CONFIRMED DISSOCIATION, NOT PROMOTED — stream-01 (positively autocorrelated) deltaSurprisal -0.2026 (helps), stream-02 (anti-correlated) deltaSurprisal +0.2433 (regresses); sign-split as predicted; ships OPT-IN behind opts.signalReserve, default path byte-identical (676/676 green)
mechanism: noveltyAmplitude(firstSeen, at, gamma) in src/core/surprise.js, fed as novelty into surpriseAt + forwardDist over the proposition field and into the figure-field Z/pNovel in reading.js; the same helper threads through src/surfer/sequence.js so the n-gram reader inherits it (one interior, two senses); cold-start falls back to the SEED NOVELTY_RESERVE; opening guards make a zero reserve well-defined (no name-snow, no NaN)
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new export noveltyAmplitude from src/core/surprise.js beside surpriseAt; wired into src/perceiver/reading.js and src/surfer/sequence.js as an OPT-IN channel (opts.signalReserve) so the default path is byte-identical
root_cause_of_gap: the constant reserve is a fixed prior over the unseen; its share novelty/(sumPrior+novelty) shrinks only as accumulated mass grows, blind to how FAST genuine newcomers arrive. A signal-derived reserve sees the rate but not the autocorrelation, so it mis-weights anti-correlated streams (a long recurrence run leaves the reserve thin right before a newcomer, and a recent newcomer wrongly inflates it before a recurrence) — the deeper gap an autocorrelation-aware reserve must close
phylogeny: consolidates a population of ~10 parallel variant PRs all proposing the same evolution: #90/92/93/97/98/104 (consensus gamma-decayed first-appearance amplitude), #95/#103 (equivalent streaming recurrence decayed=gamma*decayed+newcomers), #94 (distance-list form with explicit SEED cold-start fallback — adopted), #91 (OUTLIER: a different theory — bornNoveltyReserve = gamma-weighted FRACTION of recent steps with any newcomer, clamped to [alpha,1-alpha], wired only to the forward channel; bundled an unrelated cycle-002 adjunct/referent parser fix, left out as a separate future experiment). #93 contributed the surfer sequence.js second-sense wiring; #95/#97 the opening guards; #104 the deterministic opts.signalReserve boolean gate; #98 the central scientific finding (rate is not enough — needs autocorrelation structure), which is this experiment's verdict of record
parity: node --test tests/*.test.js 676/676 green; default readingAt + predictiveSequenceReading byte-identical (opts.signalReserve opt-in); lock added: tests/novelty-reserve.test.js
generalization: the dissociation is structural, not lexical: it holds on any one-figure-per-line stream and reproduces on the surfer sequence reader (a second front-end) — the reserve lives in the shared core, so any organ that calls surpriseAt/forwardDist/foldBefore inherits both the gain and the failure mode
omnimodal: the helper reads only first-appearance STEPS and gamma — no text, no parser — so any modality whose front-end emits a basis (tonal moves, frequency bins, cells) inherits the same rate-derived reserve and the same autocorrelation blind spot
scope_holds: signals with positively-autocorrelated or sustained novelty (bursty casts, a stream of fresh figures); any organ emitting an open basis through the core
scope_fails: anti-correlated novelty (regresses); a blanket DEFAULT (breaks byte-identical parity AND the net corpus sign is unknowable to a rate-only reserve) — hence NOT PROMOTED

capability: the omnimodal reading COUNT is signal-derived, not universal — readingCount reads how many readings a field holds as a void (deriveNull, log scale) over its eigengap spectrum, generating readings at a real elbow and dissipating to one reading (abstaining) on a flat spectrum
claim: the Born rule (assign each unit to argmax |<u|lens>|^2) is the UNIVERSAL cross-modal invariant; the segmentation COUNT is not. A geography-derived count beats both hand-rolled fixed-count rules — 90%-Born-mass (over-segments: saturates cap-12, flickers) and eigenvalues-above-the-void-floor (over-abstains on flat high-rank spectra) — on aggregate boundary F1 across a multilingual + hard battery, AND abstains exactly (zero boundaries) on every flat-geography stream
stimulus_shape: multimodal (11 scripts + 8 hard problems) · dissociation+abstention · reading-count · whole-stream · target COUNT
verdict: CONFIRMED — mean boundary F1 GEO 38% vs null 31% vs mass 20%, wins/ties 11/20, both margins clear key.min_margin (0.03); abstention exact on all 6 flat streams (noise/single/collinear/zh/ja/hi); Born assignment identical across all three readers
mechanism: readingCount(eigenvalues): leading eigenvalue gaps -> largest gap is the elbow -> kept iff it clears deriveNull over the gaps on a LOG scale (gap-spacings are heavy-tailed order statistics, voidnull's percolation setting). Cleared -> k=max(2,elbow) (a cleared gap separates >=2 reading groups); not cleared -> k=1, abstain. Born assignment to the top-k eigenlenses then segments by median-smoothed assignment switching. The SAME test both counts and abstains.
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new PURE export readingCount from src/core/voidnull.js beside deriveNull, re-exported from src/core/index.js; NOT wired into any default reader (opt-in count an omnimodal organ calls in place of a hand-set K)
root_cause_of_gap: a universal K is a number you invent: 90%-mass tracks total Born mass (blind to the elbow -> saturates the cap on any spread spectrum -> flicker), and eigenvalues-above-the-void tests each eigenvalue against the noise floor (a flat high-rank spectrum of many near-equal readings falls below it -> false abstain). The elbow is the geography both miss: the void applied to the GAP spectrum finds where a top group parts from the tail, and reads flatness as one reading.
parity: node --test tests/*.test.js 1761->1767 (6-test lock tests/omnimodal-reading-count.test.js added); readingCount is pure (no module state, no input mutation); all 1756 prior tests byte-identical
generalization: the finding is cross-lingual by construction: it holds on 8 scripts a tokenizer never sees (character-trigram front-end) and on 8 synthetic hard problems; readingCount reads only an eigenvalue array, so any organ emitting a density inherits it
omnimodal: CONFIRMED — readingCount takes a bare Born spectrum (no text, no parser): the seed/ four-sense demo (text/audio/vision/IMU) and all 20 battery streams feed the same operator; the Born assignment on top is modality-blind
scope_holds: any field whose eigengap spectrum has a resolvable elbow above its heavy-tailed gap background; any modality emitting a density; abstention on flat spectra
scope_fails: near-degenerate multiplicity (two near-equal readings) and adjacent-recurrence boundaries (A|A) are invisible to lens-switching by construction; bases too flat to separate the true readings (CJK/Devanagari char-trigrams) -> honest abstention, a FRONT-END limit the count cannot repair

capability: atmosphere (relEntropy — local density DEPARTURE) and paradigm (commutator — local INCOMMENSURABILITY) are complementary boundary channels: each detects a KIND of boundary lens-switching is blind to, and their abstention-gated union raises omnimodal segmentation over lens-switching alone
claim: lens-switching cuts where the dominant GLOBAL reading changes and is blind to (a) near-degenerate multiplicity (global lenses are mixtures) and (b) adjacent recurrence (same label, rotated subspace). Atmosphere and paradigm read the field LOCALLY (window-before vs window-after) and peak at exactly those boundaries. The union GATED by the geography abstention (readingCount) beats lens-switching on mean boundary F1 and keeps the flat streams clean; a naive ungated union regresses because change-point scores have no built-in void
stimulus_shape: multimodal (exp-0003 battery reused) · complementarity+gated-fusion · reading-count · whole-stream+local-window · target BOUNDARY-KIND
verdict: CONFIRMED — mean boundary F1 gated-union 44% vs lens 38% (atmo 30%, para 11%, oracle best-of 54%); atmosphere cracks hard_two_balls (33% vs lens 0%), paradigm cracks hard_adjacent_recurrence (40% vs lens 0%), gate keeps noise/single/collinear at 100%; all 4 key checks pass
mechanism: over the top-M reading subspace (M=2*readingCount cap 12) and window W~5% of the stream: score(b)=symmetric S(rhoL||rhoR) (atmosphere, relEntropy) or ||[rhoL,rhoR]||_F (paradigm, commutator); voidPeaks picks local maxima clearing the BOUNDED per-decision void line (boundedNull — a departure is bounded, not a heavy tail, so the log-tail bound rejects every real peak). Union of lens+atmo+para taken unless readingCount abstains -> no channel fires.
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new PURE export voidPeaks from src/core/voidnull.js beside boundedNull, re-exported from src/core/index.js; the channels reuse existing relEntropy/commutator; NOT wired into any default reader (the fusion lives in the experiment measure as the demonstrated capability)
root_cause_of_gap: lens-switching reads only the argmax GLOBAL reading, a single contrast; a field can change in ways that contrast cannot see (a density that shifts without changing its top eigenvector, a subspace that rotates without changing the assigned label). Atmosphere and paradigm are the two other contrasts the engine already computes but never pointed at time; the missing piece was a bounded-void PEAK detector (voidPeaks) so their curves fire where real and abstain where flat.
parity: node --test tests/*.test.js 1767->1772 (5-test lock tests/void-peaks.test.js added); voidPeaks is pure (no module state, no input mutation); all prior tests byte-identical
generalization: the channels read only densities (relEntropy/commutator) and a bounded score curve (voidPeaks) — no text, no parser; the finding reproduces across 8 scripts and 8 synthetic hard problems; any organ emitting a density inherits all three contrasts
omnimodal: CONFIRMED — atmosphere/paradigm take bare window densities; the same 20-stream battery (text/audio/vision/IMU generalisation) feeds them; modality-blind
scope_holds: boundaries carried by a local density departure (multiplicity, register shift) or a local basis rotation (adjacent recurrence, subspace change); any modality emitting a density; abstention preserved by the readingCount gate
scope_fails: the gate suppresses streams where the geography honestly abstains (CJK/Devanagari char-trigram bases) even though paradigm alone had signal there — a FRONT-END (basis) limit, not the channel's; the oracle gap (44->54%) shows per-stream channel SELECTION (not blanket union) is the next pressure

capability: the observed operates at MULTIPLE holon levels at once; a single global read collapses the stack, a recursive holon reader (read a scale, descend with LOCAL re-centering, re-derive the void per level, halt on abstention) recovers the finer levels, and sign-aware Born assignment (bornAssign signed) resolves the balanced splits the squared rule collapses
claim: a nested stream carries boundaries at 2-3 scales at once. Two failures stack: (1) squared |<u|lens>|^2 is SIGN-BLIND, so a balanced split centred onto one bipolar axis reads as one reading (boundary invisible) — bornAssign signed resolves it via the +/- poles; (2) even sign-aware, one global read segments only the dominant level. Recursion with local re-centering (the section's family becomes the common mode, exposing finer readings), a per-level readingCount void, and an abstention halt recovers the finer boundaries a single read cannot
stimulus_shape: synthetic nested subspaces (2-3 levels) · multi-level recovery+sign-resolution · recursive-reading · whole-stream+nested-window · target LEVELS
verdict: CONFIRMED — mean all-levels boundary F1 squared-single 36% -> signed-single 52% -> recursive-holon 65%; finer-level F1 27% -> 51% (descent nearly doubles it); coarse level reaches 100% on nested_2level_a; sign fix independently lifts hard_two_balls 0% -> 67%; all 3 key checks pass
mechanism: segMap(U): local-centre, buildDensity, eigenLenses, readingCount->k; k>1 -> bornAssign(signed) over top-k lenses, median-5 smooth, boundaries=assignment switches (NMS-merged). holons(U): segMap at scale, then recurse into each segment >=minSeg with LOCAL re-centering (depth<=2), halting where readingCount abstains. Boundaries tagged by discovering level.
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new PURE export bornAssign from src/core/spectral.js beside eigenLenses, re-exported from src/core/index.js; DEFAULT squared is byte-identical to every prior reader's argmax, signed is opt-in; the recursive reader lives in the experiment measure
root_cause_of_gap: the squared Born rule discards sign — a measurement's phase — so it cannot separate the two poles of a bipolar reading, and centering a balanced split MANUFACTURES exactly that bipolar axis; and a single ρ over the whole stream has one common mode and one reading count, so it can represent only one level of a nested object. The missing pieces were sign-aware assignment and per-level re-centering with the void as the descent's stop.
parity: node --test tests/*.test.js 1772->1776 (4-test lock tests/born-assign.test.js added); bornAssign default (squared) byte-identical; all prior tests unchanged
generalization: bornAssign reads a direction and lenses only; the recursive reader composes readingCount/eigenLenses/bornAssign — no text, no parser; holds on 2- and 3-level synthetic nests and repairs the two_balls case from the exp-0003 battery
omnimodal: CONFIRMED — pure vectors, no modality; any organ emitting a density inherits sign-aware assignment and recursive holon reading
scope_holds: streams with nested boundaries at multiple scales; balanced/bipolar splits; any modality emitting a density; abstention halts the descent at whole holons
scope_fails: finer-level precision (short noisy sub-segments keep some spurious cuts); deep nests (>=3 levels) lose F1 as per-level N shrinks and the void turns conservative — the honest floor on resolving a holon from little of it

capability: the two-way holon coupling is measurable and its arrows are DISTINCT: dynamic pull (coupling(part,whole).pull — the whole sets the part's motion) and structural constitution (bond rigidity — the part makes the whole recognizable); the rigid bonds recover the skeleton, and an autonomous-but-attached waver dissociates the arrows
claim: a part is coupled to its whole two ways. DOWNWARD (regulation): the whole's shared motion mode sets a large fraction of the part's motion (coupling(part,shared).pull). UPWARD (constitution): the part is RIGIDLY attached (constant bone length), which makes the whole a recognizable figure (bond invariance; the most-invariant partner recovers the tree). They are not one number: an off-gait waver has low pull yet full constitution, a floating ghost has constitution below every limb
stimulus_shape: synthetic articulated motion (walking stick figure) · two-arrow dissociation+structure-discovery · coupling-reading · whole-stream+kinematic-tree · target COUPLING
verdict: CONFIRMED — mean limb pull 65%, skeleton recall 100% (13/13 undirected bones), waver dissociation 3/3 (pull ~44% vs constit 100%, 56pt gap), ghost rejected 3/3 (constit 62% < all limbs); all 4 key checks pass
mechanism: coupling(part,whole)={pull=cos^2=R^2,k,residual}, part=k*whole+residual. DYNAMIC: demean node velocities, buildDensity over nodes, top eigenlens=whole's shared motion, pull=coupling(vel,shared).pull; the residual is the part's autonomy and the input to level-2 (its own shared mode). STRUCTURAL: constit=1-min_j bondCV(i,j) (std/mean of time-series distance), skeleton=argmin_j (most invariant partner)
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new PURE export coupling from src/core/spectral.js beside bornAssign, re-exported from src/core/index.js; the stick-figure measure lives in the experiment
root_cause_of_gap: the three principles were stated (low sets possibility of high, high sets probability of low, each whole at its scale) but never measured as couplings; the engine had the density (shared mode) but no atom to decompose a part into whole-set + own, and no structural (invariance) reading to tell constitution from pull. coupling is that atom; bond invariance is the structural reading; together they make the two arrows separate observables
parity: node --test tests/*.test.js 1776->1780 (4-test lock tests/coupling.test.js added); coupling pure (no state, no mutation); all prior tests byte-identical
generalization: coupling reads two signals only; bond invariance reads two position series only — no text, no parser; holds across three gaits and repairs to a clean dissociation; any organ emitting part signals + a whole reference inherits both arrows
omnimodal: CONFIRMED — pure motion vectors, no modality; any articulated/hierarchical signal (pose, a document's sections, a conversation's threads) has the same two couplings
scope_holds: any whole with parts that share a mode (dynamic pull) and are bound by an invariant relation (structural constitution); skeleton discovery where bonds are more invariant than non-bonds
scope_fails: the root reads ~0 pull (nothing above it — the shared-mode measure cannot tell 'is the frame' from 'decoupled'); structural constitution saturates at 1 for rigid limbs (separates part/non-part but does not RANK parts) — a recognisability-weighted constitution is the next pressure

capability: the GENERATE column of the operator cube realised at the spectral grain — INS (birth a reading on the void), SYN (merge a returning reading into its standing identity), REC (carry a learned reading as a prior); recognize is the branch point and REC is the keystone that closes the online loop
claim: exp-0003..0006 built Differentiate/Relate operators on rho (SEG=voidPeaks, DEF=readingCount, SIG=bornAssign, CON=coupling). The Generate column is what an ONLINE reader needs and is measurably harder: INS loses the global spectrum readingCount sees, so it births correctly only when readings are separable and under-births on overlapping/short ones. SYN merges returning readings into their identity (no re-birth churn). REC — a reading set learned from stream A carried into stream B — recognises known readings with ZERO births and beats both batch and cold online where INS is weakest
stimulus_shape: synthetic register+sequential readings · operator-realisation+regime-dependence+transfer · online-reading · streaming+cross-stream · target GENERATE
verdict: CONFIRMED — INS on seq_separable online 71% vs batch 45% (born=6=true reads, ids 6 vs batch 9); REC short-block 83% with 0 births vs batch 67% and cold 67%; all 4 key checks pass; honest limit: long-block stale prior 50% < batch 71%, and cold INS under-births short blocks (why REC is the keystone)
mechanism: recognize(u,readings,{floor=chance}) is the branch point: a match=SYN (sign-aware blend into the reading identity), a -1=novelty buffering to an INS centroid birth after a short run; REC seeds readings with lenses batch-learned from a paired stream so recognize matches from unit 1. batch=readingCount+bornAssign over global lenses
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new PURE export recognize from src/core/spectral.js (composes bornAssign), re-exported from src/core/index.js; the online INS+SYN reader and REC prior-seeding live in the experiment, not wired into any default reader
root_cause_of_gap: the Generate operators are not an oversight — Differentiate/Relate read a fixed field at once, Generate acts in TIME (birth/merge/learn) and time is where the global spectrum is lost; only a prior (REC) restores it. The missing atom was recognize (match-or-novel against standing readings), the online complement to readingCount's batch void
parity: node --test tests/*.test.js 1780->1785 (5-test lock tests/recognize.test.js added); recognize pure (composes bornAssign, no state, no mutation); all prior tests byte-identical
generalization: recognize reads a unit + standing readings only — no text, no parser; the online reader and REC prior compose it; holds on separable sequences and on a same-register transfer pair
omnimodal: CONFIRMED — pure vectors; any organ emitting units + a standing reading set inherits online birth/merge and prior recognition
scope_holds: online reading where readings are separable (INS) or known in advance (REC); recurrence identity (SYN); cross-stream transfer of learned readings
scope_fails: INS under-births overlapping/short readings without a prior (loses the global view); a stale REC prior can lose to a fresh batch read on long blocks (a prior is defeasible, not a fact); a miscalibrated floor breaks recognition and abstention together
